title: Bridge Essay: Modulation of Metacognitive Confidence Judgments Through Provision of Performance Feedback in Moderate to Severe Traumatic Brain Injury.
concept: Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Dashboard Diagnosis** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Metacognition — self-awareness, self-monitoring, neural correlates of meta-awareness.
**Phase context:** Metacognition as self-observation — neural substrate of knowing that one knows.

**Modulation of Metacognitive Confidence Judgments Through Provision of Performance Feedback in Moderate to Severe Traumatic Brain Injury.** — J Head Trauma Rehabil (2022). DOI: 10.1097/HTR.0000000000000680. PMID: 33782350. OBJECTIVE: Traumatic brain injury (TBI) may result in metacognitive impairments. Enhancing memory in healthy adults can improve metacognitive accuracy, but it is unclear whether such interventions apply to individuals with TBI. This study examined the effects of manipulating target memory experiences on metacognitive accuracy in TBI. PARTICIPANTS: Fourteen community-dwelling adults with TBI and 17 healthy controls.
